name: S1 — mixed second session

seed.subject: ban-service-consumer
seed.body: RocketMQ consumer timeout for ban-service is 60s; on timeout, retry then route to DLQ.

seed.subject: rocketmq-delivery
seed.body: RocketMQ delivery is at-least-once; ban consumers must be idempotent.

seed.subject: dlq-replay-runbook
seed.body: Write the DLQ replay runbook for ban-service.

seed.subject: reconcile-candidate-retrieval
seed.body: Use pgvector HNSW for reconcile candidate retrieval.

push.subject: rocketmq-delivery
push.body: Consumers must be idempotent because RocketMQ only guarantees at-least-once delivery.

push.subject: ban-service-consumer
push.body: Load test led us to change the ban-service consumer timeout from 60s to 30s.

push.subject: reconcile-candidate-retrieval
push.body: Candidate retrieval will use Elasticsearch kNN.

push.subject: ban-negative-cache
push.body: Negative cache hit rate is 92% in staging with a 15m TTL.

push.subject: saga-compensation
push.body: Do we need compensating actions for partial ban rollout failures?

seed_summary: Seed session: ban consumer baseline, delivery constraint, runbook step, retrieval decision.
push_summary: Second session: timeout retuned after load test, retrieval decision contested, negative-cache results.
